channel.title: Yahoo News – World
channel.description: Latest world news from Yahoo News

item.title: Footage of gas pipe explosion in Russia falsely linked to Middle East war
item.description: As the United States and Iran prepared for talks in mid-June aimed at ending the Middle East war, footage circulated online claiming to show an Israeli strike on an oil refinery in the Islamic republic. While Israel has bombed Iranian oil facilities in the past, the clip in fact depicts a gas explosion in Russia, with elements in the footage matching Yandex street view imagery of the Russian region of Kizilyurt.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Kim Jong Un inspects new weapons systems that threaten Seoul
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw tests of new weapons as part of a plan to bolster inter-Korean border firepower, state-run media reported Friday.
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: Myanmar torches $600 million in seized heroin, meth and other drugs
item.description: Thick clouds of black smoke billowed into the sky on the outskirts of Myanmar's largest city Friday as authorities burned more than 50 tons of heroin, opium, ketamine, methamphetamine, marijuana and crystal meth — some $600 million of confiscated illegal drugs destroyed nationwide. Myanmar, also known as Burma, has a long history of drug production linked to political and economic insecurity caused by decades of armed conflict. It has been a major source of illegal drugs destined for East and Southeast Asia, despite repeated efforts to crack down, and has long been one of the world's largest producers of heroin and methamphetamine.
item.dc:creator: AP

item.title: Global trade in cocaine, methamphetamine is booming, UN drug report shows
item.description: By Francois Murphy VIENNA, June 26 (Reuters) - The global trade in illicit drugs is booming, with cocaine production and seizures of methamphetamine at an all-time high, a United Nations report showed
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Former Shin Bet chief threatens to sue Channel 14 over ‘completely false’ inflammatory comments
item.description: According to the warning, Kobi had claimed during the program that Cohen had pushed for deceased Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar to be released as part of the deal to return Gilad Shalit in 2011. Former Shin Bet (Israel Security Agency) chief Yoram Cohen, who is a member of Gadi Eisenkot’s Yashar! party, sent a legal warning on Wednesday to Israel’s Channel 14 after it aired inflammatory remarks about him.
item.dc:creator: The Jerusalem Post

item.title: Dairy wins supreme title at international awards
item.description: The Wright Family Dairy is recognised among thousands of entries at the Stafford Showground event.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: Clip of building damaged by Taiwan earthquake falsely linked to Venezuela tremor
item.description: Major back-to-back earthquakes in Venezuela in June 2026 killed hundreds of people and destroyed buildings, but video of a precariously tilting structure circulating on social media was not filmed in the South American nation as posts claim. The clip in fact shows damage caused by a deadly quake that rocked Taiwan's Hualien City in April 2024.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Warning as AI clones voices and law falls behind
item.description: Experts warn AI can clone voices from short clips as UK law struggles to protect people.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: Homeless project helps 50,000 in crash victim's memory
item.description: The charity has helped thousands of people in the UK access food and drink.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: A historic heat wave catches Europe's fashion industry unprepared
item.description: As a historic heat wave gripped Paris this week, fashion houses tried to keep their guests cool with ice packs, mist machines and iced Evian on silver platters. It wasn’t enough: some venues still sweltered, water ran short and air conditioning was absent or inadequate. “I honestly thought I was going to pass out,” said Ben Freeman, a London-based fashion critic from Australia.
item.dc:creator: AP

item.title: The US-Iran war may have ended but the death toll continues to mount
item.description: On a dust-covered runway in rural Madagascar, a group of men carry a patient towards a waiting Cessna light aircraft.
item.dc:creator: The Telegraph

item.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes rises to 235, search and rescue efforts continue
item.description: The two quakes struck in quick succession, just minutes apart, according to the US Geological Survey, marking it one of the South American nation’s worst disasters in over a century.
item.dc:creator: Euronews

item.title: Neighbors dig through Venezuela rubble to search for loved ones after 2 deadly earthquakes
item.description: In cities across northern Venezuela, neighbors helped each other dig through rubble to search for loved ones on Thursday after back-to-back earthquakes that officials say killed more than 230 people and left thousands injured the night before. The official death toll rose to around 235 late Thursday, with at least 4,300 people injured, Venezuela Health Minister Carlos Alvarado told state media. The 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes that struck Wednesday evening was among the strongest in Venezuela in more than a century and was felt throughout the region.
item.dc:creator: AP

item.title: EU warns Turkey over 'unacceptable' snub of Cyprus in climate summit preparations
item.description: LUXEMBOURG, June 26 (Reuters) - The European Union has rebuked Turkey for excluding Cyprus from preparations for this year's U. climate summit, as diplomatic tensions ‌mount over the issue ahead of the global climate talks.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Sydney beaches under shark alerts for fourth day as sightings continue
item.description: Sydney beaches, including the world-famous Bondi Beach, have been repeatedly closed over the past four days following a series of shark sightings off Australia's largest city, authorities said on Friday. Lifeguards and drones spotted sharks again on Friday morning near Tamarama and Bronte beaches in Sydney's eastern suburbs, according to the New South Wales government's SharkSmart platform.
item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: Mexico's World Cup joy muted in regions gripped by cartel violence
item.description: Much of Mexico has been celebrating the World Cup with joyful outdoor gatherings, filling streets, plazas and fan zones in the country's three host cities — Mexico City, Guadalajara and Monterrey — since the tournament kickoff on June 11. In villages and towns across Mexico where shootings are a near-daily occurrence, the cheers are mostly confined indoors. There, real-life concerns outrank the excitement around a World Cup in which Mexico has reached the knockout stage.
item.dc:creator: AP

item.title: Vandalised Colombian church clip falsely linked to Israeli soldiers' conduct in Lebanon
item.description: Video documenting smashed statues and religious figures inside a church was filmed in Colombia, contrary to posts that falsely linked it to previous cases of Israeli soldiers desecrating Christian symbols in southern Lebanon. The same footage previously circulated in posts and reports about the vandalism of La Ermita church in the Colombian city of Cali.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Power outages, fuel bans and no summer camps: Ukraine steps up pressure on Russia by targeting Crimea
item.description: Energy shortages have continued to plague Sevastopol, the largest city in Russian-controlled Crimea, following repeated, intensified Ukrainian strikes on the peninsula.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: India’s viral youth movement has moved from memes to the streets. Their leader tells CNN why
item.description: Protesters from the satirical Cockroach Janta Party are camped out in India’s capital, refusing to leave until the education minister resigns over a national exam scandal.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: French teen in Singapore straw-licking case to enter plea
item.description: A French teen facing criminal charges in Singapore for a straw-licking stunt will enter a plea next month in a case that could land him in jail on conviction, a court heard Friday.His lawyer told a district court on Friday that his client -- who was not present at the hearing -- would enter a plea on the afternoon of July 13.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Forever wars: Israel’s cycle of conflict shows no finish line
item.description: Despite ceasefires and shifting military fronts, Israel’s wars continue, with no clear end or lasting security gains.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: Ali Khamenei’s secret bunker included blast-resistant room, escape tunnels under Tehran
item.description: Khamenei’s underground bunker had become an IDF target during Israel’s strikes against Iran in March, with clandestine IDF units 8200 and 9900 providing intelligence on its location. Former supreme leader Ali Khamenei’s underground bunker complex included a blast-resistant room and escape tunnels beneath central Tehran, Iran International reported on Thursday, citing architectural plans received from an informed source.
item.dc:creator: The Jerusalem Post

item.title: 'Unbearable': tracking heat in one of New Delhi's poorest areas
item.description: Durga Devi finds no relief after a day working in New Delhi's sweltering summer, because her poorly ventilated home radiates trapped heat, leaving her bedroom as hot as 45 degrees at night."I prefer staying outside after work, because inside the house there is no relief," said 45-year-old Devi, who lives in the cramped lanes of Delhi's Sundar Nagri district.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Middle East war sends Asia racing toward solar as oil shock upends energy security
item.description: "Power outages happen almost every day so I don’t want to depend on the grid."
item.dc:creator: The Cool Down

item.title: Australia considers tougher enforcement of social media ban for teens
item.description: Australia's prime minister said on Friday he was keen to make sure the country's social media ban for children was as strong as ‌possible, as a new study found that the measure — the first of its kind ‌and now six months old — had little impact on teen use. "What we want to do is to make sure that the laws are as strong as possible and that they will withstand any legal challenges which are made," Prime Minister Anthony Albanese told the Australian Broadcasting Corp. He added that one key focus would be making ‌sure that the eSafety Commission, the ⁠country's internet regulator, was sufficiently empowered to do the job.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: Firefighting plane crashes in northern Canada; 3 killed
item.description: Authorities in northern Canada on Thursday confirmed that three people are dead after a plane crashed in the Northwest Territories the night prior.
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: Viral bullying videos test Bhutan's digital transition
item.description: In the Himalayan kingdom of Bhutan, which has long limited outside influence, viral videos of school bullying have triggered debate over social media's erosion of traditional values.Filmed by classmates as others looked on, the videos showed secondary school girls slapping and punching another student to the ground.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Trump administration pledges $150M in aid, deploys Navy warships after deadly Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Trump administration pledges $150 million in aid and deploys U.S. Navy warships to Venezuela after deadly earthquakes kill at least 235 people.

item.title: Video Shows Car Plowing Into World Cup Fans in Mexico, 17 Injured
item.description: Check out this video, filmed by TikTok user @verodejesus4 in Cabo San Lucas ... fans were out celebrating Mexico's World Cup victory over Czechia Wednesday night when a black car suddenly barreled through the packed street. Seventeen people were hurt, and authorities say the driver was detained at the scene before being taken to a hospital in police custody.
item.dc:creator: TMZ

item.description: A cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz was attacked Thursday, prompting officials to halt evacuating sailors stranded in the chokepoint by the war.

item.title: Iran says only approved Hormuz routes are safe as UN halts evacuation
item.description: Iran warned on Thursday that ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz should use only routes designated by Tehran following an attack on a vessel off the coast of Oman. Routes not approved by Iran were not covered by its guarantee of safe passage, the Port and Maritime General Services Authority (PGSA), the Iranian body responsible for the Gulf, said in a post on X.

item.title: NZ internal report warns of Chinese military forays in Pacific
item.description: Chinese ballistic missile tests and naval forays will become a "persistent" feature of the Pacific as Beijing expands its security presence, New Zealand officials said in an internal document obtained by AFP. The warning was issued in a December 2025 report by New Zealand's Defence Force and Ministry of Foreign Affairs while they were monitoring a People's Liberation Army navy flotilla in the Philippine Sea.New Zealand's Defence Force did not identify the type of one of the naval vessels deploye

item.title: More health centres needed as Congo's Ebola death toll passes 300
item.description: The number of Ebola deaths in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has passed 300, officials said late on Thursday, as the outbreak continues to spread within the region. There are currently 1,155 confirmed cases of Ebola in Congo, the Information Ministry in Kinshasa reported.

item.title: France, Italy seek coalition to support Lebanon after UN mission ends
item.description: France and Italy will seek to build an international coalition to support Lebanon after the current United Nations peacekeeping mission UNIFIL ends, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and French President Emmanuel Macron announced. "As you know, the UNIFIL mission ends at the end of the year, and we believe it is necessary to ensure an international presence that prevents an extremely dangerous security vacuum," Meloni told a news conference after a bilateral summit in Antibes on Thursday.

item.title: Plan To Move Ships Through Strait Of Hormuz Paused After Iran Strikes Cargo Vessel

item.description: UPS announced a 'Ship and Dip' discount to help World Cup fans visiting the United States get their new favorite dressing home.

item.title: Popular squishy toys seized after similar products test positive for known carcinogen
item.description: Officials say children have suffered burns after toys reportedly burst or leaked hot contents when heated to make them softer.

item.title: North Korea's Kim calls for 'destructive' military posture as South vows to boost drones
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un observed major weapons tests and called for the military’s “deadly and destructive offensive posture” to be bolstered, state media reported Friday, as South Korea announced it would drastically boost its drone warfare capabilities. The development comes as tensions remain high on the Korean Peninsula, with Kim pushing to strengthen both nuclear and conventional military capabilities while refusing to return to talks with South Korea and the United States. The tests watched by Kim on Thursday were meant to evaluate the power of a “special mission" warhead for a tactical ballistic missile, an upgraded multiple rocket launch system and the hit accuracy of shells with an extended firing range of a self-propelled gun-howitzer, the official Korean Central News Agency said.

In an Instagram video that quickly racked up millions of views Thursday, Alexander Lunin addressed the Russian president to warn of “very serious consequences” if his demands were ignored. The 39-year-old father of two went on to say he’d been asked to “deliver a message” to Putin by unnamed military officials an

item.title: King Charles Dramatically Breaks With Biggest Royal Tradition
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla have made a bombshell announcement. They have formally declared that they will never move into Buckingham Palace—an end of 189 years of official royal residence at one of the world’s most famous buildings. The announcement, made as part of a financial statement the couple’s aides published Thursday, may confirm what has been an open secret to insiders for years, but is a seismic moment which upends a tradition started by Queen Victoria, his great-great-great-grandm

item.title: Israel reports killing six Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli forces killed six members of the Hezbollah militant group in two separate incidents inside a self-declared "security zone" in southern Lebanon on Thursday, the Israeli military said. The military said the men had posed a threat to its soldiers but did not provide further details.

item.title: King Charles will not live in Buckingham Palace after renovation
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla will not move into Buckingham Palace once its nearly £370 million ($488 million) refurbishment is completed. The famous building will remain the operational centre of “monarchy HQ” but Charles has decided for his reign it will not be his official residence, and nearby Clarence House will remain his London home.

item.title: UK'S King Charles breaks precedent to reveal £30 mn paid in taxes since 2022
item.description: King Charles III has paid more than £30 million ($39.6 million) in personal taxes since becoming monarch in September 2022, Buckingham Palace announced Thursday, in the first such disclosure by a reigning British sovereign.It noted in the two full tax years since he acceded to the throne on September 10, 2022, his personal bill was £24.6 million.

The 23-year-old from Gravesend, Kent, has been charged with the premeditated murder of a 26-year-old British man she met online. George, however, claims she acted in self-defence after enduring repeated physical abuse during their relationship. item.title: Things to know about the Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: A rare double earthquake ravaged Venezuela on Wednesday, killing at least 188 people and leaving more than 200 trapped. Thousands of people have been reported missing and about 1,500 people have been injured. The powerful 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes struck 39 seconds apart along the San Sebastian fault on Venezuela’s northern coast, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.

item.title: Hormuz evacuation effort on hold after new ship attack
item.description: The vessel was attacked after Iran warned ships must use its designated corridors through the Strait of Hormuz rather than the route the U.S. has backed.

item.title: Metro explosion video is from 2021, not earthquake in Venezuela
item.description: Two major earthquakes that devastated Venezuela on June 24, 2026, killing dozens and leaving survivors battling to rescue loved ones trapped beneath the rubble of collapsed buildings, have also prompted a surge of old and unrelated videos spread out of context on social media. For example, one widespread clip was falsely claimed to show the quakes causing explosions on a Caracas metro, when the images were actually from an incident in 2021.

item.description: A major music festival in Germany was temporarily interrupted on Thursday after fires broke out outside the event grounds, though organizers resumed the event around two hours later after firefighters brought the situation under control. The Fusion Festival in the north-eastern state of Mecklenburg-Vorpommern was suspended shortly before 7 pm (1700 GMT), according to police.

item.title: What to know about earthquake early warning systems
item.description: As earthquakes struck from California to Venezuela to Japan, millions of people received warnings on their mobile phones, providing critical seconds to seek protection. Venezuela was struck with a pair of deadly earthquakes Wednesday evening and Thursday morning that brought significant shaking to the northern coast of Japan.

item.title: UN agency halts Hormuz evacuation operation after ship attacked
item.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) has temporarily suspended its evacuation operation in the Strait of Hormuz following an attack on a vessel in the Gulf of Oman, the UN agency said on Thursday. "I have decided to temporarily pause its implementation in order to reconfirm that the necessary safety guarantees continue to be in place for the ships on our evacuation list and all those in the region," IMO Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez said in a statement.

item.title: Paris bans sale, consumption of alcohol amid record heat
item.description: Paris police have temporarily imposed a ban on alcohol in the French capital as record temperatures stretch the health system to its limits. From Friday until Sunday morning, the sale of alcohol is prohibited and the consumption of alcohol in public spaces is banned, the police said on Thursday.

item.description: At least 188 people have died in Venezuela in two back-to-back earthquakes, the president of the National Assembly, Jorge Rodríguez said on Thursday. A further 1,520 people have been injured, and more than 200 are trapped under the rubble.

item.title: New attack on cargo vessel in Strait of Hormuz

item.title: At least 188 dead in Venezuela earthquake, many more injured, missing
item.description: Large earthquakes hit Venezuela Wednesday evening and killed at least 188 people, authorities said, with 1,520 injured and 157 missing.

item.title: Turkish journalism groups say independent outlets denied accreditation for a NATO summit in Ankara
item.description: Several independent Turkish media outlets were denied accreditation to cover an upcoming NATO summit in Ankara, journalism groups said Thursday, calling the decision an affront to media freedoms. U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to join other leaders from the 32-member alliance for the July 7-8 summit in the Turkish capital, during which allies will, among other issues, debate defense spending and attempt to project unity. Turkish journalists from news organizations viewed as opposition‑leaning or independent — including Halk TV, Sozcu TV, Cumhuriyet newspaper, T24 news website and ANKA news agency — have been excluded from covering the summit, the Turkish Journalists' Association and other media solidarity groups said.

item.title: Fuel-price shock ignites rooftop solar boom in the Philippines and Thailand

item.title: The 2 earthquakes that struck Venezuela are known as a 'doublet.' Here's how they happen
item.description: The two powerful earthquakes that struck Venezuela’s northern coast, killing more than 180 people, were an event known as a “doublet.” Doublet earthquakes happen when a pair of similar-sized quakes hit close in location and time, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. On Wednesday evening, a 7.2 magnitude quake hit first, followed by a magnitude 7.5 just 39 seconds later.

item.title: Yemeni journalist killed in a car bombing after reportedly receiving warnings

Mohamed Eida, a correspondent for the Saudi-backed television networks Al-Arabiya and Al- Hadath, was killed late Wednesday when an explosive device planted in his car detonated in the port city of Mukalla in Hadramout province, according to the broadcaster. The network said local authorities had warned him about a month ago that his life was at risk.

item.title: Oil tankers pass through the Strait of Hormuz despite Iran threats as traffic slowly picks up and oil prices fall
item.description: A number of oil tankers sailed out of the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday along a United Nations-recommended route, defying threats by Iran that any ship not following its approved route “will be dealt with accordingly.”The passage of the Liberian-flagged vessel — perhaps aptly named the Stoic Warrior — came amid continued uncertainty about the future of the crucial waterway.

item.title: One dead and child injured as train hits car
item.description: A child is in hospital with life-threatening injuries following the incident in Hoghton.

item.title: EU releases 3 billion-euro loan package for Ukraine's recovery as part of 2-year commitment
item.description: The European Union has disbursed the first 3 billion-euro ($3.4 billion) tranche of a 90 billion-euro ($101 billion) loan to Ukraine, the country’s prime minister announced Thursday at the opening of a conference on Ukraine's post-war recovery in Poland. The conference, attended by key European leaders including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, is both a fundraising forum and a message to Russia that Ukraine’s Western supporters are in it for a long haul.

item.title: Venezuela president: Death toll from earthquakes rises to at least 164
item.description: The death toll from the two severe earthquakes that hit Venezuela has climbed to at least 164, with at least another 971 injured, acting President Delcy Rodríguez told the VTV broadcaster on Thursday. Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes with magnitudes of 7.

item.title: 164 killed in Venezuela after back-to-back earthquakes. What to know
item.description: Back-to-back major earthquakes struck Venezuela's northern coast on June 24, raising fears of widespread deaths across the region.

item.title: UK moves to criminalise gender and sexuality conversion therapies
item.description: LONDON, June 25 (Reuters) - Britain on Thursday set out plans to criminalise abusive and harmful therapies intended to change a person's sexual orientation or gender identity.

item.title: Venezuela's 7.5-magnitude quake strongest in more than a century
item.description: The 7. 5-magnitude earthquake that struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening was the strongest to hit the South American country since 1900, according to data from the US Geological Survey (USGS).

item.title: Germany offers support to Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
item.description: The German government on Thursday offered to supply support to Venezuela after the South American country was hit by two major earthquakes, leaving scores of people dead. "The news of the terrible earthquake in Venezuela has left us deeply saddened.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Why is Central America so vulnerable to tremors?
item.description: Venezuela and Japan are both hit by strong quakes, but Venezuela faces higher risks due to weaker building standards.

item.title: Riyadh’s RCRC begins fourth phase of road development at $2.6bn
item.description: Planned works target four major Riyadh corridors, aiming to ease congestion and boost daily road network capacity.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes live updates: Dozens dead, hundreds injured after 2 quakes
item.description: Two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening.

item.title: ICJ lawyer Philippe Sands to receive Peace Prize of German Book Trade
item.description: French-British author and human rights lawyer Philippe Sands will be awarded the Peace Prize of the German Book Trade for his "unwavering defence of international law," the German Publishers and Booksellers Association announced on Thursday. Sands, 65, regularly serves as counsel at the International Court of Justice (ICJ) in The Hague.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Russia is shifting air defenses to Moscow and other key sites after drone strikes
item.description: Russia is moving a significant part of its air defenses to protect a handful of prime targets, including Moscow, as Ukraine’s long-range drones continue to hammer sites deep inside the country, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says. Ukraine has in recent months stepped up its aerial campaign against Russian military installations and energy facilities. Its success has caused fuel shortages and disrupted army supply lines, stalling Moscow’s invasion of its neighbor after more than four years of fighting and rattling Russian officials.

item.title: Canadian workers have few protections against workplace surveillance as TD starts monitoring
item.description: Canadian employees have few legal protections against workplace monitoring, experts said, after TD Bank told some staff it would use software to track their work activity, raising concerns about consent and privacy. There are limits, however, to ‌the reasons employers monitor their workers and how they use the data they collect. Canada's privacy laws are weaker than those in the ‌European Union, where privacy is a fundamental right and companies must justify the use of monitoring tools under privacy and labor laws, according to six Canadian legal experts.

item.title: Old clip falsely shared as recent rival party clash at Pakistan's National Assembly
item.description: Oppos ition legislator Shahid Khattak was reportedly involved in an altercation with a lawmaker from Pakistan's ruling party in June 2026, but a video falsely claiming he slapped a member of Pakistan Muslim League Nawaz (PML-N) during parliament deliberations is unrelated. The video is from 2025 and shows Khattak's partymate, Khalid Nisar Dogar, striking a member of the Punjab provincial assembly.

item.title: Back-to-Back Quakes Rattle Venezuela: What to Know
item.description: High casualties are expected after an ‘earthquake doublet’ with magnitudes above 7.

item.title: Russia’s War On Faith: The Toll On Ukraine’s Religious Communities
item.description: Since February 2022, Russia’s full-scale invasion has devastated Ukraine’s religious communities. Sacred sites have been destroyed, and clergy members have been killed.

item.title: Two years after Gen Z uprising, Kenyan protesters return to Nairobi streets
item.description: Two years after Kenya's 2024 Gen Z tax revolt shook the nation, protesters gathered in Nairobi on 25 June to seek justice for victims and press for greater government accountability.

item.title: At Least 32 Dead, Over 700 Injured as 2 Earthquakes with Magnitudes of More Than 7 Hit Venezuela: 'Disaster Zone’
item.description: The US Geological Survey (USGS) said the initial earthquake had a magnitude of 7.1, followed by an even larger tremor of 7.5 which hit moments later

item.title: Island prison to be investigated by UK inspector
item.description: Home Affairs vice-president John Gollop says "we actually need to know what the issues are".

item.title: Police arrest two suspected of pulling gun, threatening haredi protesters blocking Highway 1
item.description: Jerusalem police arrested two suspects after a video surfaced showing an alleged firearm threat toward Haredi protesters blocking Highway 1. Officers from Israel Police's Jerusalem District arrested two suspects during the overnight between Wednesday and Thursday, due to suspecting that they drew a gun and threatened haredi (ultra-Orthodox) protesters blocking Highway 1 during Wednesday's protests.

item.title: China defends patrols east of Taiwan after 3 European nations raise alarm
item.description: China on Thursday defended its recent patrols in waters east of Taiwan, one day after Britain, France and Germany expressed alarm about what they described as “novel Chinese activity." While the three European countries said the activity, which they did not specifically identify, endangered regional stability, a Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said China's law-enforcement and patrol activities were aimed at maintaining regional stability and maritime order. China deployed coast guard ships in response to an announcement by Japan and the Philippines that they would discuss their maritime boundaries in waters that Beijing views as its own.

item.title: Three men charged with murder after stabbing
item.description: Kamahl Cameron-Williams, 31, was stabbed in Westow Road during the early hours of Sunday 21 June.

item.title: Mossad rehearsed 2018 Iran nuclear archives raid in Africa, former chief Yossi Cohen reveals
item.description: The operation changed the course of history as it was the impetus for US President Donald Trump to pull out of the 2015 Iran nuclear deal shortly after the operation's results were revealed. Former Mossad chief Yossi Cohen has revealed for the first time that Africa was the geographic area where spies from his agency carried out their "dress rehearsal" for the now famous operation in which they broke into Iran's secret nuclear archives in January 2018.

item.title: Germany offers to send military planes to Venezuela after quakes
item.description: German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius has offered military assistance to Venezuela after the South American country was hit by two major earthquakes, which are feared to have left scores of people dead. "The Bundeswehr is on standby and can make up to six A400M transport aircraft available at short notice as soon as a request for support is received," Pistorius said in his ministry’s WhatsApp channel on Thursday.

item.title: EU limits visas for Somalis over lack of cooperation on deportations
item.description: The European Union has decided to restrict visa provisions for Somali nationals after an EU report concluded that Somalia is not cooperating sufficiently on deportations of its nationals. The temporary restrictions mean that Somali nationals will no longer receive multiple entry visas and that the standard processing period for visa applications is extended from 15 to 45 days, a press release published on Thursday said.

item.title: Israel, Lebanon deny that Israel has withdrawn from part of southern Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT, June 25 (Reuters) - Senior Israeli and Lebanese officials denied on Thursday that there had been any Israeli withdrawal from occupied southern Lebanon, after a U.S. official said Israel had

item.title: Beaten and starved: Shock in India as police rescue men from bonded labour
item.description: Bonded labour was outlawed in India in 1975, but it still continues in villages and small towns.

item.title: Russia reports deaths after Ukrainian strikes on occupied Crimea
item.description: Ukraine has launched fresh attacks on the Russian-annexed Crimean Peninsula, killing at least two people, according to the Moscow-appointed authorities in the region. Sergey Aksyonov, the Moscow-appointed head of Crimea, said one of the victims was a minor.

item.title: British TikTok influencer charged with murder in Dubai
item.description: A British TikTok influencer has been charged with murdering her lover in Dubai.

item.title: IDF soldier dies, additional soldier injured, after vehicle overturns during operation in Lebanon
item.description: Chief Sgt. First Class (res. ) Basil Sweid, 32, from Peki’in, was a driver in the 75th Battalion of the 7th Armored Brigade.

item.title: World reacts as devastating Venezuela earthquakes kill at least 32
item.description: Global leaders express solidarity with Venezuela as dual earthquakes kill at least 32 and leave widespread devastation.

item.title: Iran warns against Hormuz crossings without authorisation
item.description: An oil tanker navigates the strait, reportedly hugging the western coast, despite threats from Revolutionary Guards.

item.title: Fabricated quotes targeting Rohingyas in Malaysia circulate online
item.description: A controversial cattle slaughter incident has reignited anti-Rohingya sentiment in Malaysia, with posts sharing fabricated statements online claiming Rohingyas are demanding their own territory in the Southeast Asian nation. AFP found one of the quotes was attributed to a non-existent Rohingya leader whose purported picture was likely AI-generated, while the other is from a bogus Instagram post of a US-based Rohingya man who denied making the remarks.

item.title: Hong Kong bookshop owners arrested in free-speech crackdown
item.description: Two owners of one of Hong Kong’s last independent bookshops were arrested on Wednesday in the latest attack on freedom of speech in the city.

item.title: Iran war day 118: IRGC rejects new Hormuz route; Rubio to meet GCC leaders
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard warns against alternative route in Strait of Hormuz as top US diplomat to meet Gulf leaders.

item.title: Naples area jolted by 3.6-magnitude earthquake
item.description: A 3. 6-magnitude earthquake rocked the region near the southern Italian city of Naples in the early hours of the morning on Thursday, the National Institute of Geophysics and Volcanology (INGV) reported.

item.title: Poland buys V-BAT drones to boost Baltic Sea naval operations
item.description: The deal puts Poland in line to become the first country on NATO’s eastern flank to acquire the Shield AI-made drone.

item.title: Photos of Muharram: a sacred Islamic month for spiritual reflection and resolutions
item.description: Muharram is the first month of the Islamic calendar and one of four sacred months during which Islam forbids warfare and encourages increased prayer, charity and reflection. Muslims welcomed a new lunar year in what’s known as the Hijri calendar. The Hijri New Year, beginning on the first day of the month of Muharram, signals a chance for spiritual reflection and religious resolutions.

item.title: Residents describe terrifying moment twin quakes hit Venezuela
item.description: Venezuelans have described panic, destruction and desperate escapes after two violent earthquakes hit the country within a minute on Wednesday evening. "I've never been so scared in my life; it was terrifying," a 57-year-old woman told dpa from the eastern part of the Venezuelan capital Caracas, which is just under 200 kilometres from the epicentre of the quakes.

item.title: Kenya police in massive show of force on protest anniversary
item.description: Kenyan police put on a massive show of force Thursday in a bid to prevent protests marking two years since a landmark Gen Z uprising.But they paid a heavy price, with at least 127 people killed across the two years of protests, according to a police watchdog, as security forces shot people with near-total impunity.

item.title: Trump offers to help ‘great friends’ in Venezuela after devastating earthquakes leave dozens dead
item.description: Addressing the situation in Venezuela, President Trump claimed that ‘early reports are not good’ amid estimates thousands may have been killed

item.title: Higher penalties, more time to prosecute: Child sexual abuse victims welcome new EU directive
item.description: New rules greenlighted this week will cover the use of AI in child abuse cases and considerably extend the limitation period to a minimum of 32 years.

item.title: ‘We hugged each other and ran’: Venezuelans recount earthquake horror
item.description: Survivors describe panic and collapsing buildings during two strong quakes as thousands of people feared dead.

item.title: Popular Ibiza DJ named as alleged 'mastermind' behind international drug trafficking operation
item.description: Popular Ibiza DJ Hugo Bianco has been named in a major Spain-US drug trafficking case. He is the alleged “mastermind” of an international drug trafficking network, and faces a nine-and-a-half prison sentence.View on euronews

item.title: Police seal off key roads in Nairobi as Kenya braces for Gen Z protests
item.description: Protesters demand justice for more than 80 people killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

item.title: Do you know these five ways to reduce the risk of a shark attack?
item.description: A recent shark mauling off Sydney's famous Coogee Beach left a 35-year-old mother with life-threatening injuries and an amputated arm, renewing the greatest fear of many people who swim in open water. Although fatal shark attacks are rare, when they do occur they often make headlines - such as the death of a woman in California in December 2025 and a 12-year-old boy in Sydney in January 2026.

item.title: King cobra hides under bedroom floor, triggering hours-long rescue operation
item.description: To avoid hurting the animal, he said, he tried to draw it out with water and smoke.

item.description: Russia and Ukraine are still in the "first stages of combat testing" drone AI that can decide to attack a target on its own, said Beskrestnov.

item.title: Powerful back-to-back earthquakes strike Venezuela, kill at least 32
item.description: At least 32 people were killed and around 700 others injured after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, acting President Delcy Rodríguez said early on Thursday, with casualty numbers set to rise further. No casualty figures were yet available from the particularly badly affected state of La Guaira on the Caribbean coast, which Rodríguez described as a "disaster area.

item.title: At least 32 killed and 700 injured in powerful Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Residents of the capital Caracas were forced to evacuate their residences and seek shelter outdoors as the quakes, which struck less than 200 km from the capital, caused widespread damage.

item.title: French aversion to air conditioning melts as homes sizzle
item.description: As Europe's heatwave leaves millions sweltering in poorly insulated apartments, schools and retirement homes, more French people are breaking with tradition to turn to air conditioning.These included modern buildings with large glass windows, or relatively new homes with insulation that worked in winter but not in summer, he said.

item.title: Deadly twin quakes are a gut punch to a Venezuelan economy already on its knees
item.description: The strongest quakes to hit Venezuela in more than a century, measuring up to magnitude 7.5, could not have come at a worse time for the country.

item.title: Trump-linked resort shines light on Albania's 'stolen' land
item.description: Acres of land that Albanian Minella Balliu has fought for decades to reclaim has been thrust into the global spotlight by the Trump family's plans to build a luxury hotel complex on it.While thousands take to the streets chanting the slogan "Albanian is not for sale", Balliu has taken to the courts in hopes of recovering his ancestor's land.

item.title: An oil tanker navigates the Strait of Hormuz despite threats from Iran's Revolutionary Guard
item.description: A Liberian oil tanker made its way out of the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday despite threats to shipping from Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard and using a new route close to Oman’s shore that has been promoted by a U.N. maritime agency. From getting ships through the narrow mouth of the Persian Gulf to the future of Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium, the two nations are increasingly debating the terms of the deal signed last week. Through the signing of the memorandum of understanding, the U.S. and Iran agreed to a 60-day period to iron out these and other details.

item.title: The Dutch are now killing children and nobody seems to care
item.description: Once a fringe idea, euthanasia is having its moment internationally and in the United Kingdom. Lauren Edwards is reintroducing the Leadbeater bill with every intention of ramming it through Parliament by using the threat of the Parliament Acts.

item.title: Mumbai monsoon floods streets and disrupts travel
item.description: Heavy monsoon rains flood Mumbai, submerging roads, disrupting traffic and forcing residents to wade through waterlogged streets in India’s financial hub.

item.title: ‘Salami slicing’: How China is trying to increase control in the Pacific
item.description: In a span of just a few weeks, China’s ships have carried out “law enforcement” activities farther from its mainland than ever before, mapped a highly sensitive seabed and conducted “research” inside a highly contested lagoon more than 500 miles from its shores.

item.title: Kenyan police block roads around the country's capital ahead of anti-government protest
item.description: Police in Kenya set up roadblocks on Thursday to prevent access to the capital, Nairobi, ahead of a planned demonstration marking two years since at least 60 people died in anti-government protests that also saw protesters storming the parliament. Families whose loved ones died in the 2024 protests have said they would protest the delay in justice for the victims. The government has been accused of a lack of transparency in an ongoing process to compensate those who suffered human rights abuses during the protests.

item.title: Chad sees influx of drone victims from Sudan
item.description: Faouzi Abdulbagi Mahammat Anour woke up in Tine, Chad, with a burnt face and his right eye missing after being attacked on the Sudanese side of the border by a drone."Since early May, drone strikes have intensified around Tina, a city on the Sudanese side of the Chad–Sudan border," said medical charity Doctors Without Borders, which is known by its French acronym, MSF.  This has led to repeated influxes of wounded patients arriving at Tine hospital in Chad.

item.title: Venezuela rocked by 7.5, 7.2 earthquakes: What happened and what we know
item.description: Rescue efforts under way as Venezuela declares a state of emergency after powerful twin quakes cause widespread damage.

item.title: Robert Thurman, Buddhist monk who after finding enlightenment became a scholar-advocate of Buddhism in the West
item.description: Robert Thurman, who has died aged 84, was regarded as the first Westerner to be ordained a Tibetan Buddhist monk; he subsequently became a leading scholar of Buddhism in America, helping to popularise practices such as meditation and yoga, as well as advocating for the Tibetan cause, one lent glamour by the Hollywood sheen of his actress daughter, Uma.

item.title: Kafr Qasim girl's organ saves Jerusalem boy in life-saving heart transplant
item.description: “Organ donation gives life, literally. Whoever saves one life, saves an entire world,” said the doctor in charge of the transplant.

item.title: London Transport Museum to get makeover
item.description: T﻿he Covent Garden attraction, which welcomed 450,000 visitors last year, will remain open throughout.

item.title: Pakistani Hindus join Shiite Muslims in Muharram observances honoring Imam Hussein
item.description: Raju Rathore’s Hindu faith and devotion to Imam Hussein converge in Pakistan’s port city of Karachi during Muharram, one of Islam’s sacred months. Religious observances during this period are primarily associated with Shiite Muslims.

item.title: Venezuela reeling after powerful twin earthquakes kill at least 32 people as promises of aid pour in
item.description: Powerful earthquakes that struck Venezuela in quick succession Wednesday evening killed at least 32 people and injured 700, the nation's acting president said, as communities across the South American country sustained damage. Acting President Delcy Rodríguez warned the toll was expected to rise as rescuers search collapsed buildings and emergency crews reach devastated areas after the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes that struck shortly after 6 p.m. Rodríguez declared a state of emergency in an address to the nation late Wednesday and said the quakes caused damage in several states.

item.title: Shiite Muslims collectively mourn to mark Muharram and Ashoura
item.description: Shiite Muslims have been observing a solemn period of communal mourning leading to the day of Ashoura, which marks the seventh century killing of Hussein, a revered leader and a grandson of Prophet Muhammad. Annual day of Ashoura commemorations are observed on the 10th day of the month of Muharram in the lunar-based Islamic calendar. In Shiite communities, Ashoura is viewed as a symbol of struggle against injustice and tyranny.

item.title: Trump says US ready to help Venezuela after major earthquakes
item.description: US President Donald Trump has promised swift assistance to Venezuela after two powerful earthquakes struck the South American country on Wednesday. Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform that the two major earthquakes were "massive in scale" and had left "a devastating number of deaths.

item.title: Shiite Muslims mark holy day of Ashoura after months of war in Iran and Lebanon
item.description: Shiite Muslims around the world on Thursday marked Ashoura, a holy day symbolizing sacrifice and martyrdom that holds special significance for many this year after months of war in Iran and Lebanon. Ashoura commemorates the martyrdom of Imam Hussein, grandson of the Prophet Muhammad, at the Battle of Karbala in 680 A.D. Imam Hussein was killed with his family and companions after refusing to pledge allegiance to the Umayyad caliphate. The event cemented the schism between Sunni and Shiite Islam and remains a powerful symbol of resistance against oppression and injustice.

item.title: Video Of Littered Street In Paris: What We Know
item.description: Does a viral video of a littered sidewalk accurately represent a typical street in Paris? It does show a street in the French capital, but it's a rather remote location known for law enforcement raids against unauthorized flea market vendors....

item.title: Rescuers race to find survivors as Venezuela reels from massive quakes: Everything we know so far
item.description: Venezuela has suffered its worst tremors in a century, with the scale of damage likely to be significant.

item.title: Gdańsk donor conference to discuss the reconstruction of Ukraine
item.description: Against the backdrop of a dispute between Warsaw and Kiev, international donors will meet in Gdańsk from Thursday to discuss the reconstruction of Ukraine. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky will be represented by Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko.

item.title: A cancer patient’s cats inspired a push for pets in hospice wards
item.description: When Ewa Lutka-Krawczyk was diagnosed with gallbladder cancer, her first thought was for Gaja, a shelter dog she took in three years ago. Left at home with Lutka-Krawczyk's husband, Gaja was barely eating. Under proposed new legislation in Poland, patients like Lutka-Krawczyk soon would have the right to be visited in hospices and palliative care wards by their pets.

item.title: Two Powerful Earthquakes Strike Venezuela, Many Casualties Feared
item.description: Both quakes measured over 7.0 magnitude, with tremors being felt as far as more than a thousand miles away in Brazil’s Amazon region.

item.title: Mothers search, men weep amid debris of Venezuela quakes
item.description: "Antonio, it's your mom, I'm right here," a mother's desperate cries bounced off the rubble of a 22-story building in Caracas that collapsed following two powerful earthquakes in Venezuela on Wednesday.At around 6pm local time (2200GMT), two earthquakes struck the same part of Venezuela in quick succession.

item.title: Confirmation still a rite of passage in Denmark but less Christian
item.description: At 15, like most Danish teenagers, Nora Pihl chose to get confirmed but for her, as for a growing number of young Danes, it wasn't a religious affair.And although Nora chose to forgo the traditional religious confirmation, she still wore a white dress, the customary attire for the rite of passage.

item.title: Peace Prize of German Book Trade winner to be announced in Frankfurt
item.description: The winner of the Peace Prize of the German Book Trade will be announced in the city of Frankfurt on Thursday morning, the German Publishers and Booksellers Association has said. The prestigious award, which carries a prize of €25,000 ($28,382), honours individuals who have contributed to the realization of the idea of peace through literature, science or the arts.

item.title: Australian woman and daughter to return from Syrian camp for IS families under strict conditions
item.description: The last Australian woman held in a Syrian camp for families of Islamic State group fighters has been given permission to return to Australia under strict conditions, Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said Thursday. The woman and her nine-year-old daughter had planned to return to Australia in February with a group of Australian women and children held in the Roj camp, but was prevented from leaving by a temporary exclusion order. Australia created the orders in 2019 to prevent defeated IS fighters from returning from the Middle East for up to two years.

item.description: By Renju Jose SYDNEY, June 25 (Reuters) - Australia faces multi-faceted threats from autocratic regimes, hackers and antisemitic extremists that present a systemic challenge to national security and

item.title: Venezuela declares emergency after earthquakes cause major damage
item.description: Venezuela's government declared a state of emergency after two powerful earthquakes caused widespread damage across parts of the country, acting President Delcy Rodríguez said late on Wednesday. Rodríguez announced the measure in a televised address, describing the situation as an incident with serious consequences.

item.title: This Indian state is trying to ensure no one grows old alone
item.description: Kerala is searching for new ways to care for its ageing population as migration leaves more elderly people living alone.

item.title: Baseball broadcasts capture frightening Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Dual earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday, leading to scary scenes in the South American country. And the moments were captured live during Venezuelan baseball league broadcasts. Senadores de Caracas was playing Marineros de Carabobo in a Venezuelan baseball league at Estadio Universitario de Caracas. The stadium holds over 20,000 people and was built in 1952.…

item.title: Study: Most Australian young people still on social media despite ban
item.description: Eight in 10 Australian young people reported still accessing social media in the months after a ban for under-16s took effect, according to research. The findings from a small study of the initial impacts of the world-first ban suggested "insufficient evidence of any substantive early effects", the University of Newcastle, Australia, study said.

item.title: Beloved spiritual utopia under threat in Modi's India
item.description: It was founded as a Sixties utopia "where men and women of all countries are able to live in peace and progressive harmony" without money or religious strife in southern India.India is a spiritual country, and we are blessed by that.

item.title: Cambodia’s Supreme Court to rule on treason appeal of 2 journalists jailed for photos
item.description: Cambodia’s Supreme Court was expected to decide Thursday on the appeal of two journalists convicted of treason for posting photographs related to border clashes with Thailand last year. Phorn Sopheap of Battambang Post TV Online and Pheap Pheara of TSP 68 TV Online were arrested last July while returning from reporting trips to the border on allegations they posted photographs they took in a restricted military zone on Facebook. One image showing land mines was widely republished by Thai media outlets and bolstered Thailand's claims that Cambodia had laid new mines along the border that wounded patrolling Thai soldiers.

item.title: IDF soldier found handcuffed in West Bank town
item.description: A preliminary investigation found that the soldier was unarmed and had entered the West Bank with criminal intentions. Judea and Samaria police rescued an IDF soldier from the West Bank town of Tarqumiyah, following a report from security forces of a person in military uniform handcuffed in the center of the town.

item.description: Two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela within less than a minute on Wednesday evening, the US Geological Survey (USGS) said, shaking the capital Caracas and causing damage in several areas. The USGS measured the quakes at magnitudes 7.

item.description: Does the opening clip of a video with a screaming woman in a hot air balloon show footage from the accident in Brazil where an "entire family" was killed? No, that's not true: That clip shows Casey Cleaver during a...

item.title: How one Indian textile worker copes with extreme heat in factory work and cramped living quarters
item.description: Before dawn breaks over the Indian industrial hub of Surat, textile worker Sibaram Pradhan is already awake, sitting on the floor in a cramped room he shares with as many as nine other men. Sweat beads on his forehead even at 6 a.m. as sweltering heat and humidity make days and nights hot across India this summer. Like numerous others from his home state of Odisha in eastern India, Pradhan works in a power loom factory that produces polyester cloth in Surat, among the largest hubs for synthetic fabrics in the world.

item.title: New military service draws more young people to German armed forces
item.description: Applications to join Germany's armed forces have risen sharply since the launch of a revamped military service programme aimed at boosting troop numbers, the Defence Ministry said on Wednesday. Germany has received about 38,500 applications across all military career tracks so far this year, up 24% from the same period in 2025, while enlistments rose 13% to around 11,000, the ministry said.

item.title: 12-year-old boy American boy attacked by shark while vacationing in the Bahamas
item.description: A 12-year-old boy was injured after being bitten by a shark while swimming in the Bahamas. The boy's mother told the Royal Bahamas Police Force that they were on a tour of the Exuma Cays, and the boy was swimming with his brother when the shark attacked him.

item.title: Thousands feared dead after double earthquake in Venezuela
item.description: Two of the strongest earthquakes in Venezuela’s history rocked the country’s north on Wednesday evening, trapping residents as buildings cracked and crumbled.

item.title: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes slam Venezuela, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas
item.description: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes slammed Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas. The U.S. Geological Survey said the first earthquake had a magnitude of 7.1 and its epicenter was west of the community of Morón, located along the country’s Caribbean coast, about 168 kilometers (104 miles) west of Caracas. The quake had a depth of 13 kilometers (8 miles).

item.title: Why Do Some Russian Fighter Jets Have Tires On Wings?
item.description: Tires on the wings of a jet may seem like an odd choice, but Russia is putting wheels on top of their fighters for a very smart defensive reason.

item.title: Zimbabwe Senate approves bill to extend presidential term
item.description: Zimbabwe's Senate on Wednesday overwhelmingly approved controversial constitutional amendments that would keep President Emmerson Mnangagwa in office until 2030, giving the changes full parliamentary support after earlier consent from the lower house.Senate president Mabel Chinomona said 75 senators voted in favour and four against.

item.description: One of Scotland's leading gangland figures has arrived in Spain after losing a bid against his extradition on a series of charges related to organised crime.

item.title: Zelensky: Russian drone signal boosters in Belarus switched off
item.description: Russian drone signal boosters in Belarus have been switched off following an ultimatum from Kiev, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Wednesday. The equipment ceased operating on Monday, Zelensky was quoted as saying by Ukrainian media.

item.title: Russian opposition politician sent to penal camp over social media
item.description: Russian opposition politician Maxim Kruglov has been sentenced to seven years in a penal colony on charges of spreading false information about the military, Russian media reported on Wednesday, less than three months before parliamentary elections. Kruglov, deputy chairman of the liberal opposition party Yabloko, was convicted over two social media posts published in 2022.

item.title: Zimbabwe's Senate approves bill to delay the presidential election and overhaul the vote
item.description: Zimbabwe ’s Senate on Wednesday approved constitutional amendments that would remove direct presidential elections, delay the next one and extend the tenure of the country’s 83-year-old leader, whose signature is the final step for them to become law. Seventy-five senators voted in favor of the bill that would postpone elections scheduled for 2028 to 2030 and extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term by two years. The bill overhauls the way presidents are chosen, replacing direct popular elections with selection by lawmakers.

item.description: Hong Kong police have arrested two people for allegedly selling items "with seditious intent", authorities said early Thursday, with local outlets reporting one of the items was a biography of jailed media mogul Jimmy Lai.The two people arrested "are suspected of displaying items with seditious intent and selling publications with seditious content inside the shop, including materials inciting hatred against the... government, the judiciary, and law enforcement agencies", police said.

item.description: A fragile ceasefire between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah militia held on Wednesday despite isolated violations in southern Lebanon, as Lebanese and Israeli officials continued talks aimed at shoring up the truce. The Israeli military said it had killed two Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon, describing them as a threat to its forces.

The restrictions will run from 3 p.m. local time Wednesday through 7 a.m. Thursday, overlapping with Mexico’s […]

item.title: Nigeria's Senate passes bill to allow creation of state police to tackle insecurity
item.description: The Nigerian Senate on Wednesday approved a bill to allow states to have their own police forces, paving the way for a major reform that would reshape the policing structure of the conflict-racked nation. The proposed constitutional change will decentralize the country's police force, which is currently controlled by the federal government, but has been overstretched by an escalating insecurity crisis. The state police forces will operate alongside the federal police.

item.title: One of Iraq's largest oil fields suspends operations
item.description: One of Iraq's key oil fields has been forced to suspend operations. The operator of the West Qurna-2 field in the southern province of Basra said on Wednesday that crude oil production and storage activities had been completely halted, citing the ongoing force majeure situation in the region and a shortage of available oil tankers.

The dramatic response, captured on video and shared to X, has viewers debating whether he went too far. Video sparks debate over how far business…

item.title: Tusk pledges continued support for Ukraine despite historical spat
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk has pledged continued support for Ukraine despite an ongoing historical dispute between the two countries. Despite the emotions involved, Poland stands by Ukraine in its confrontation with Russia, Tusk said at a meeting of the leaders of Germany, France, Britain, Poland and Italy in Berlin on Wednesday evening.

item.description: The Nigerian Senate on Wednesday approved a bill to allow states to create their own police forces, following lower chamber passage earlier this month of what could be a potentially sweeping security reform.- Police to 'fill gaps' - Supporters of the reform say it would create nimble police forces that are more easily held accountable and staffed by locals who know the terrain.

item.title: Brazil detains Spanish citizen in the latest detention of a foreigner for racism
item.description: Brazil’s federal police detained a Spanish citizen in Sao Paulo’s international Guarulhos airport for racism on Wednesday, the latest in a series of high-profile arrests of foreign tourists on similar grounds. Brazil, which has grappled with its legacy of slavery, has some of the strictest anti-racism laws in Latin America, with protections enshrined in the 1988 constitution. The airline company said that there was no justification for the aggression directed at its employees and condemned all forms of racism and discrimination.

item.description: The European Union is planning a military and civilian support mission for Lebanon's armed forces and police aimed at helping the country extend state authority across its territory, according to a concept paper seen by dpa on Wednesday. The proposal, drafted by the EU's diplomatic service, envisages a mission lasting at least three years to improve the operational capabilities and readiness of Lebanon's security forces through advice, training and capacity-building.

item.title: Berlin memorial commemorates Jehovah's Witnesses persecuted by Nazis
item.description: A memorial to commemorate Jehovah's Witnesses persecuted by Germany's Nazi regime was inaugurated in a Berlin park on Wednesday, 81 years after the end of World War II. “This memorial is dedicated to the people who suffered bitter injustice and yet time and again demonstrated humanity,” said Julia Klöckner, president of the Bundestag, during the unveiling ceremony in the Tiergarten park.

item.title: Suspected Russian-linked gas-supply sabotage prompts raids in Germany
item.description: German federal prosecutors are investigating a Russian national on suspicion of attempting to sabotage Germany's gas supply following the start of Russia's war against Ukraine, with a former subsidiary of state-owned Russian energy group Gazprom at the centre of the case. On Wednesday, officers searched premises belonging to the suspect and a second person, who is not a suspect, in Berlin, Germany's supreme prosecuting authority said on Wednesday.

item.title: First ships pass through Strait of Hormuz under UN evacuation scheme
item.description: The first ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz under a UN-backed operation to evacuate thousands of seafarers and restore maritime traffic after weeks of disruption, the International Maritime Organization (IMO) said on Wednesday. The UN shipping agency announced plans on Tuesday to evacuate 11,000 seafarers from the region.

item.description: Police divers have discovered more than half a million dollars worth of hashish hidden in a cave on the picturesque Italian island of Ponza.

item.description: A new study suggests most SUV buyers are not swayed by warnings that larger vehicles pose greater risks to cyclists and pedestrians. Researchers say awareness campaigns alone may not change behavior and tougher measures could be needed.

item.description: Germany on Wednesday unveiled a monument to Jehovah's Witnesses persecuted and murdered by the Nazis -- a group that experts say has been historically overlooked in commemorations of the Holocaust.Around 14,000 Jehovah's Witnesses –- at least 10,700 Germans and 2,700 from European countries occupied by Germany -- were persecuted by the Nazis, mostly in the form of imprisonment.

item.title: France sees first Ebola case detected in Europe during Congo outbreak
item.description: France has confirmed the first Ebola case detected in Europe since the start of the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, health authorities said on Wednesday. A humanitarian aid doctor who returned from the Central African country tested positive for the virus, the French Health Ministry said.

item.description: ZURICH, June 24 (Reuters) - Switzerland has started contract negotiations with manufacturers from France, Israel and South Korea for a ‌second air defence system following delays to its order of U. ‌Patriot missile systems, the government said on Wednesday.

item.title: Microsoft, Germany's BKA and Europol take down 200 cybercrime servers
item.description: Investigators from Europol, Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (BKA) and US tech giant Microsoft have dealt a major blow to organized cybercrime, taking down more than 200 command-and-control servers used by criminal hackers to remotely control infected devices. At the same time, criminal control over more than 18,000 identified victim computers worldwide was severed, they said on Wednesday.
